title: "Color in 'The Masque of the Red Death'"
creator: Lipke, Veronica
description: Analytical essay about color as a motif in "The Masque of the Red Death" by Edgar Allen Poe. This essay is 3 pages long and was the first essay Veronica wrote for a college-level English course. She still had some work to do as a writer......
subject: essay; color in literature; gothic fiction (literary genre); symbolism--fiction

title: "Mother Stands for Comfort"
creator: Lipke, Veronica
description: Prose analysis essay analyzing the personal narrative, "Without a Map" by Meredith Hall. This essay is 4 pages long and was written while Veronica was still trying to decide on her major.
subject: essay; creative nonfiction; symbolism; travel in literature

title: "The Blood of a Traitor: Blood as Betrayal and Guilt in 'Macbeth' and 'Red Queen'"
creator: Lipke, Veronica
description: Analytical essay exploring the utilization of blood as symbolism in William Shakespeare's "Macbeth" and Victoria Aveyard's "Red Queen," 7 pages. After writing this essay, Veronica decided to declare her major in English.
subject:
essay; blood---symbolic aspects; Shakespeare, William, 1564-
1616---symbolism

title: "Temporality, the Consequences of Labor, and The Spectre Haunting Europe: The Intersections of Ideology in 'Sir Gawain and the Green Knight'"
creator: Lipke, Veronica
description: Critical research essay applying Marxist theory to "Sir Gawain and the Green Knight," 14 pages. This was Veronica's final essay written for the Literary Theory course she took during her first semester as an English major.
subject: essay; criticism; marxist literary criticism; semiotics and literature

title: "Final Reflection: Masculinity in Roman Identity"
creator: Lipke, Veronica
description: Analysis of lines 1.3.1-14 of William Shakespeare's "Antony and Cleopatra," involving an analytical lens of Roman patriarchal values and toxic masculinity depicted in the play; 3 pages. This essay was written as a complement to a group presentation on the topic of the poor treatment of women of color in politics.
subject: essay; patriarchy in literature; masculinity in literature

title: "Love and the Physical in Sappho’s '[Like the very gods]'"
creator: Lipke, Veronica
description: Research paper and close-reading of Sappho's "[Like the very gods]," 8 pages. This essay is the final draft after having been worked on for the entire spring 2023 semester.
subject: essay; criticism; sapphic poetry; love poetry

title: "Intersections of Exploitation in Environmental Literature"
creator: Lipke, Veronica
description: Literary meditation on intersections of human and environmental exploitation as depicted in Robin Wall Kimmerer's "Braiding Sweetgrass," Rebecca Roanhorse's "Trail of Lightning," and Zora Neale Hurston's "Their Eyes Were Watching God," 11 pages. This essay was written for an environmental literature course that was very influential for sparking Veronica's interest in Indigenous knowledge systems and decolonial criticism, which she continued to carry as she pursued an MLIS with a career in archives.
subject: essay; criticism; environmental literary criticism; indigenous knowledge systems; American literature---African American authors

title: "Materiality, Memory, and Community: How 'Nineteen Eighty-Four' Constructs the Past"
creator: Lipke, Veronica
description:
Short essay meditating on the role of history and the past in George Orwell's "Nineteen Eighty-Four," 5 pages. This was written for a final essay assignment, wherein a series of prompts were posed by her professor for the students to choose from. Here, Veronica chose to answer the prompt: "Why is
the past more important in ["Nineteen Eighty-Four"] and what kinds of past matter?"
subject: essay; criticism; marxist literary criticism; history in literature; memory as a theme in literature

title: "Race Formation, Antisemitism, and Colonialism in Frank Norris’ 'The Octopus'"
creator: Lipke, Veronica
description: Research paper analyzing complications surrounding the antisemitic tropes and western expansionism depicted in Frank Norris' "The Octopus," 10 pages. This essay was an expansion of the midterm essay she wrote for this course.
subject: essay; criticism; antisemitic literature; blood accusation; colonial affairs

title: "'Hangover' by Yesika Salgado"
creator: Lipke, Veronica
description: Close-reading of the poem, "Hangover" by Yesika Salgado, 3 pages. This was written as the final paper for this course's unit on "Instapoetry" as a literary form.
subject: essay; feminist poetry; alcoholism in literature; poetry, modern
